Video.summary: You've heard people complain, "If I only knew then what I know now, I wouldn't be in this mess," but what about those times when you choose not to see the writing on the wall? Dr. Phil meets couples who say their relationships went from bad to worse after they said, "I do," and now their families are broken.
Video.title: How to Fix a Broken Family

Image.alt: How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family

Video.summary: What happens when an unhealthy teen relationship threatens to tear a mother and daughter apart? Giesel says her 18-year-old daughter, Devynn, is obsessed with her boyfriend and is damaging a once promising future. Watch the drama unfold as a pregnancy scare rocks this teen's life.
Video.title: Teen Love Trauma

Video.summary: A teen girl so obsessed with her on-again, off-again boyfriend that he claims she has become a stalker, forcing him to get a restraining orderů for three years! Cristie says her 19-year-old daughter Corryn started dating 17-year-old Jack almost two years ago and every time they break up, Corryn becomes a ôcrazy ex-girlfriend.ö Jack claims Corryn calls him hundreds of times a day, breaks into his house and hides behind the bushes to watch his every move, each time violating the restraining order. But Corryn says Jack plays games with her and lures her to him every time. Cristie and Jackĺs mom, Brytt, say theyĺve had it and want their teensĺ relationship to end today -- but what does Corryn have to say about that? Can she stop obsessing over Jack, and will she keep a promise she made to Dr. Phil?
Video.title: A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex

Image.alt: A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex

Video.summary: A Meddling Mother-in-Law or a Lying Cheating Daughter-in-Law? Dr Phil delves into the family drama.
Video.title: Toxic and Offensive Inlaws

Video.summary: From spending $600,000 on 3-year-old beauty queens, to pushy stage sisters and an ambitious boxing coach Ś today's guests say they go to the extremes to help the youngest members of their family excel. Meet two fathers, one whose full-time job is managing his tiny stars' careers, and the other who hand makes each and every one of his 4-year-old daughter's costumes. Are these dads bonding with their daughters or simply pushing too hard? Plus, meet several women who defiantly criticize child beauty pageants. And, hear from one overzealous sister and the father of a boxing champion once ranked first in the world. Can Dr. Phil convince these guests to step aside and strive for balance in their children's lives?
Video.title: Competing For The Title

Video.summary: Parents express frustration with their twin sons' disrespectful behavior.
Video.title: Big Time Moochers!

Video.summary: A woman believes she's going to be Vladimir Putin's wife.
Video.title: Homeless and ôEngagedö to Putin?

Video.summary: In part two, will accused liar Sarah, who fibbed about having cancer, miscarriage and muscular dystrophy, change her ways?
Video.title: Liar,Liar: Sarah Faces Her Accusers

Video.summary: If you think pretty people are immune to heartache, think again. The headlines are filled with stories of striking women who were devastated by infidelity, despite their outward beauty. Dr. Phil sits down with Joey, a husband and father, who says he canĺt pass up a pretty face. His wife, Shannon, says heĺs slept with close to 100 women since their wedding day. The personal trainer justifies his actions by saying Shannon is overweight and not hot enough for him. Will he get a wake-up call about his cheating ways? Or will his wife take him back yet again? See the shocking text messages to Joeyĺs mistress, and hear Dr. Philĺs strong message for the hapless husband when a disturbing secret is revealed. Can Dr. Phil encourage Joey to choose another path and look beyond the physical?
Video.title: Beauty Isnĺt Enough

Video.summary: A mother describes her daughter's self-destructive behaviors.
Video.title: 16 and on the Path of Destruction

Image.alt: 16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction

Video.summary: Demi Moore did it. So did Madonna and Halle Berry. This trend of older women dating younger men is called the Cougar Craze, and itĺs sweeping the country. Recently, at a New York speed-dating event, "Sugar Mamas and Boy Toys," good-looking 35-year-old men wooed women over 35 who made at least $500,000 a year. Jeremy organized the event and says he was just answering a demand from affluent ladies. Was he sincere in his matchmaking or just out to make a buck? Dr. Phil talks to two women who participated in the event -- Gail, 44 and Nancy, 50. Their experiences might surprise you. Then, 51-year-old Kat says sheĺs no cougar; she just likes to date younger guys because men her age are set in their ways. She says her 23-year-old ex-boyfriend, Ryon, was loving, passionate and had a much higher libido than older men. Her friend, Nancy, says Kat has a great head for business, but a bad one when it comes to the opposite sex. She says young men just see dollar signs when they see Kat. Is Kat dating down, or should Nancy just butt out? And, Bobbi had an 11-year relationship with a man who was 17 years her junior, but she says once she turned 50, he saw her as too old. She wants to be in a relationship again, but not with an older man. Her son, Nathan, says someone still wet behind the ears canĺt appreciate all Bobbi has to offer. Does Nathan have the right to put his foot down with his mother? Do you think it's OK for older women to date younger men? Take our poll!
Video.title: The Cougar Craze

Video.summary: The conversation continues with a woman who says her sister lied about health diagnoses for attention.
Video.title: Saving Shemida: Whatĺs The Truth?

Video.summary: We've all heard of in-laws who don't get along, but these guests say their problems have become scary and even life-threatening.
Video.title: In-Law Threats!

Video.summary: Marcy and Scott say they believe their daughter, Danielle, has Stockholm syndrome from her boyfriend, Justin. They claim Justin is an abusive narcissist who has manipulated their daughter into believing heĺs ôGodĺs gift to the world.ö Danielle says she loves Justin, and heĺs not going anywhere. She says the only reason Justin has ever laid hands on her is to restrain her from her own angry rages, and her parents donĺt understand their relationship. Justin says heĺs a great guy with an ôX factorö and doesnĺt understand why Danielleĺs parents dislike him. Although heĺs currently dealing with a serious legal problem, he says heĺs not a criminal, narcissist, or an abuser. And, in a shocking twist, Danielle reveals to Dr.Phil and her parents a secret about her relationship with Justin.
Video.title: Hula Hooper's Bad Boyfriend

Video.summary: Dr. Phil debates viewers who disagree with the advice he gave to previous guests. One woman feels he had no right to push his view of adoption on pregnant 15-year-old, Alexandra. Another woman accuses Dr. Phil of bullying the participants on the Weight Loss Challenge and even accuses him of disliking fat people! What does Dr. Phil say about her inflated accusations? Then a 16-year-old bi-racial girl disputes Dr. Philĺs advice to a mother facing a racial identity crisis. How will these viewers fare when they go head to head with Dr. Phil?
Video.title: Debate Dr. Phil

Video.summary: Jessica says her mother, Patricia, claims she is defying the odds by becoming a record-setting weight lifter while simultaneously battling Stage 3 brain cancer. But Jessica insists her mother isnĺt sick at all and that she is lying about her health to gain sympathy and attention. She says Patricia has been spinning stories about various medical ailments for as long as she can remember -- and itĺs time to come clean. Patricia strongly denies her daughterĺs accusations and insists her diagnosis and incredible survival story are real. Why does she claim Jessica is out to get her? Emotions run high when mother and daughter meet on Dr. Philĺs stage. Do Patriciaĺs claims hold up? And, can this mother and daughter repair their fractured relationship?
Video.title: Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?

Image.alt: Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?

Video.summary: In the summer of 2014, a former middle school English teacher was arrested on suspicion of statutory rape and contributing to the delinquency of a minor after police found her in a motel room with a 15-year-old former student. In a Dr. Phil exclusive, Jennifer Caswell speaks out for the first time. How does she explain her relationship with her former student?
Video.title: Teacher Scandal Confession

Video.summary: Things get even more heated when Wanda is accused of stealing her daughter in-law's wedding rings. Does Wanda have the rings? And can Dr. Phil get this family communicating for the first time in the right way?
Video.title: Good, Bad, and Wandaĺs Bi-Polar

Video.summary: Dr. Phil digs further into the heartbreaking story of a father's desperate search for his missing daughter, Kimberly Medina, and the loss he felt when his grandchildren were taken away by the man who may be guilty of their mother's disappearance. It's a shocking story that has had a ripple effect on three generations. Don't miss this chilling tale that will have you hugging your loved ones a little tighter.
Video.title: Was Mommy Murdered?

Video.summary: The nation was captivated in 2009 when Susan Powell, a beautiful loving mother of two, vanished without a trace. Her husband, Josh, was considered a person of interest but was never charged with a crime. His account of what happened the night Susan disappeared raised more questions than answers, and what began as an unsolved mystery spiraled into a custody battle, which tragically ended last Sunday with the horrific murder-suicide of Josh Powell and their two innocent sons, Charles, 7, and Braden, 5.
Video.title: Powell Family Tragedy

Video.summary: The contentious 2020 presidential election has shown that thereĺs a clear divide in America ľ half the country is celebrating, and the other half is angry ľ making the upcoming holiday season especially tricky to navigate. Instead of being a time of joy, celebration and giving thanks, many families are faced with the dilemma of how not to argue with their own spouses, children and parents about politics. Dr. Phil meets a Republican father, who is barely speaking to his Democrat daughter after an argument they had over Donald Trump. Then, Dr. Phil meets another father, a lifelong Democrat, who says he raised his son to be a free thinker ... but never imagined that heĺd support Trump! The election is officially over, but the drama remains. Whatĺs Dr. Philĺs advice on how to move forward post- election without turning holiday dinners with family into a war zone over politics? Tune in!
Video.title: 2020 Election: Families Divided

Video.summary: Dr. Phil continues his look at a teacher sex scandal that has a school and families divided in a small town in Texas. High school teacher Joelle Ogletree faces accusations of sexual misconduct with some of her male students. Hear both sides of this gripping sex scandal from the accuser and the accused, and judge for yourself if justice has been served.
Video.title: Teacher Sex Scandal (PT. 2)

Video.summary: Would you rather be right or happy? Dr. Phil talks with some right-fighters who can't settle their debate.
Video.title: I Am Right!

Video.summary: Secrets are revealed in a family with a shopaholic wife and rageaholic husband.
Video.title: Family Secrets, Family Lies

Video.summary: A mom is supposed to love and nurture her children, but what if she's too involved? Reed, 33, says his mother, Margaret, is so meddling, manipulative and controlling that the day she dies will be a cause for celebration, not mourning. He claims she uses bribes to control his every move, including offering him $20,000 to divorce his first wife. Margaret admits that she can be pushy and difficult but says she just wants to see her son succeed in life. Is she really setting him up for failure? Does Reed really want to man-up and break free from his mother's grasp? And, is Margaret willing to let him go? Then, 35-year-old Anthony, star of the new TLC reality show Mama's Boys of the Bronx, says he sees nothing wrong with living at home and letting his mom, Patti, wait on him hand and foot. How does Patti feel about their living arrangement? Will she ever kick Anthony out? Plus, hear how Anthony's lifestyle affects his dating life. If you know of a mother-son bond that's a little too close for comfort, don't miss this Dr. Phil. Reed, 33, says his mother, Margaret, is so meddling and controlling that the day she dies will be a cause for celebration. Margaret says she wants to see her son succeed - but is she setting him up for failure? Plus, 35-year-old Anthony, star of TLC's Mama's Boys of the Bronx, says he sees nothing wrong with living at home.
Video.title: Over The Top Moms

Video.summary: Some believe self-censorship is a big problem today, particularly in light of the idea that someone can be "canceled" for their words -- blocked from having a prominent public platform or career for sharing their opinion. Dr. Phil interviews Nate, a former Idaho police officer, who claims he was fired after making a TikTok video about NBA superstar LeBron James that went viral and has been viewed over 4 million times. Nate explains how the fall-out from the video affected him and his family, too. Dr. Phil also speaks with conservative political commentator Tomi Lahren, who says she recently had to be protected by state police from student protestors at the University of New Mexico. Greg Lukianoff, president of the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression, says he believes Americans have lost the ability to discuss their differences rationally. And, Dr. Shaun Harper, founder and Executive Director of the USC Race and Equity Center, says he is firmly against hate speech and feels people need to be more mindful about the effects of their words.
Video.title: You Canĺt Say That! Pt 1

Video.summary: ôHe loves me. He loves me not.ö Wouldnĺt it be great if there was a formula to get your partner to commit? Josie has been dating Curry for more than three years, and she says sheĺs ready to dump him if he doesnĺt pop the question soon. Curry says he loves his girlfriend, so why is he afraid to walk down the aisle? Will Josie hold out for a ring, or will she kick this commitment-phobe to the curb? Then, Melissa thought she had it all ľ- the ring, the dress and the man of her dreams ľ- until she discovered her wedding was a sham. She says her husband, John, lied about how many times heĺd been married, but the biggest tale of all is that heĺs still married to another woman! John admits that he lied about being divorced, but says Melissa wouldnĺt have stayed with him if he told her the truth. Will Melissa ever be able to trust him again ... and should she? Plus, meet a couple who says their dream wedding turned into a nightmare when the wedding planner scammed them out of $30,000.
Video.title: Commit Or Quit

Video.summary: TV Star Nicholas Brendon has been starring in his own private alcohol-fueled drama which has led to multiple arrests and been documented in celebrity magazines, social media and blogs. Nicholas sits down with Dr. Phil -- and abruptly walks offstage. Is Nicholas ready to take the steps needed to save his life and career?
Video.title: TV Star's Alcoholism Spirals

Video.summary: A 7-year-old boy's knives fascination and violent imaginary friend injured teachers.
Video.title: Our 7yo Threatens to Kill Us!

Video.summary: Do slow drivers make you crazy? Do you start to lose it when cell phone talkers are too loud? People are more on edge than ever, with the slightest thing setting them off.
Video.title: Anger Management

Video.summary: Samantha and her sister Sarah say their mother, Stacy, is very successful as a special education teacher and behaviorist, but hey claim their mother has allowed their 33 year old brother Ben to live rent free for the past 2 years, while he lays by the pool. Stacyĺs husband, Doug, says he moved out and refuses to come home until Stacy kicks his stepson out of the house. Ben insists he is not taking advantage of his motherĺs hospitality and kindness; he is just stuck in a rut.
Video.title: Mom Needs to Cut the Cord

Image.alt: Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord

Video.summary: Moms are accused of bullying their own children.
Video.title: Bully Moms
